commit.message:
fix(ClosingQuoteView): use canonical URL for social share links

LinkedIn and Facebook only support passing a URL to their share dialogs
(text pre-fill is not supported). Both platforms pull title, description,
and image from the page's OG tags.

Replace window.location.href with the hardcoded canonical URL so shares
always point to the production page and OG tags are reliably scraped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Tune lwIP: 20 TCP PCBs, 8 UDP PCBs, 2s TIME_WAIT

- MEMP_NUM_TCP_PCB: 8 → 20 (prevents connection refused under load)
- MEMP_NUM_TCP_PCB_LISTEN: 4 (explicit listener slots)
- MEMP_NUM_UDP_PCB: 8 (ready for UDP protocol)
- TCP_MSL: 1s (2s TIME_WAIT vs 40s default — fast PCB recycle on LAN)
- BSS cost: +2KB (374→376KB, 144KB free)
- Fix bench_run.py empty result handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix gap below reply button when reply box is collapsed

Collapsed .reply-row had flex: 0 0 100% which claimed its own flex
line; the container's row-gap then added 6px of blank space after the
buttons even when the reply box was hidden.

Setting flex: 0 0 0% when closed removes it from the flow entirely.
flex: 0 0 100% is restored when the .open class is toggled on.

commit.message:
Fix skill activation: SessionStart hook + @federation agent

Problem: Squad's coordinator agent (squad.agent.md) intercepts all user
messages before plugin skills activate. 'Set up a team organization'
triggers Squad's Init Mode, not our federation-setup skill.

Solution (combined approach):
- Option A: SessionStart hook injects federation awareness into the
  coordinator's context. Lists specific topics to delegate to federation
  skills instead of handling as normal Squad init. Works immediately.
- Option B variant: @federation agent for explicit invocation. User can
  say '@federation set up a team organization' to bypass the coordinator
  entirely. Always available, no competition.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Idempotent: skips pages with existing JSON output unless --force
- Validates JSON output, saves raw response on parse errors for debugging

- Can process single or multiple volumes (glob-friendly)

commit.message:
fix: prevent zombie downloads from untracked transfers in previous runs

Three changes to download.py:

1. Pre-run stale cleanup (defense-in-depth)
   Before enqueueing, cancel any Queued/Paused/Errored/Cancelled leftovers for
   the same filenames from a prior failed run (include_active=False preserves
   concurrent InProgress downloads).  existing_ids is snapshotted first so stale
   IDs are naturally excluded from the post-enqueue diff regardless of slskd
   removal timing.

2. Abort-path untracked sweep (primary fix)
   All three abort paths (speed-check, queue-timeout, inactivity-timeout) now
   also call _cancel_transfers_for_filenames with skip_ids=active.values().
   This catches transfers that were enqueued but whose ID was never discovered
   by _find_new_transfer_id — those were silently left in slskd's queue and
   could resume downloading later as 'zombie' transfers.
   _cleanup_incomplete_files now receives both tracked and untracked filenames.

3. Smarter fallback ID resolution
   The filename-based fallback now prefers transfer IDs not in existing_ids
   (newly created) before falling back to the last-by-position entry, reducing
   the chance of latching onto a stale transfer from a previous run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: reorganize imports and clean up unused code across multiple… (#410)

* refactor: reorganize imports and clean up unused code across multiple files

- Reorganized import statements for clarity and consistency in `worker_hft.ts`, `init.ts`, and various task files.
- Removed unused variables and types in `RerankerTask.ts`, `StructuralParserTask.ts`, and `AiTask.ts`.
- Updated type definitions in vector storage implementations to enhance clarity and maintainability.
- Cleaned up commented-out code and unnecessary functions in `IndexedDbTable.ts` and other utility files.
- Improved overall code readability and structure without altering functionality.

* refactor: reorganize imports and enhance input schema in DocumentEnricherTask and HierarchicalChunkerTask

- Moved import statements for clarity in `DocumentEnricherTask.ts` and `HierarchicalChunkerTask.ts`.
- Updated the input schema for both tasks to include `type: "object"` and `additionalProperties: true` for the `documentTree` field.
- Cleaned up unused code in the test files by removing commented-out variables and improving overall readability.

* refactor: enhance input schema for DocumentEnricherTask and HierarchicalChunkerTask

- Revised the TODO list to reflect current priorities and next steps, including the introduction of an AI agent compound task.
- Enhanced the input schema for `DocumentEnricherTask` and `HierarchicalChunkerTask` to include `documentTree` as a `DocumentRootNode`, improving type safety and clarity.
- Cleaned up import statements for better organization in task files.

* refactor: enhance vector storage implementations with TypedArrayConstructor support

- Added support for specifying a TypedArray constructor in various vector storage classes, including `InMemoryVectorStorage`, `IndexedDbVectorStorage`, `PostgresVectorStorage`, `SqliteAiVectorStorage`, and `SqliteVectorStorage`.
- Updated the `CreateKnowledgeBaseOptions` and `CreateSqliteKnowledgeBaseOptions` interfaces to allow for a customizable vector type.
- Improved type safety and flexibility in the `createKnowledgeBase` and `createSqliteKnowledgeBase` functions by utilizing the new vector constructor parameter.
- Cleaned up import statements and ensured consistent usage of the new vector type across relevant files.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: update vector type parameter in createKnowledgeBase options

- Renamed `vectorType` to `vectorCtor` in the `createKnowledgeBase` function and `CreateKnowledgeBaseOptions` interface for clarity and consistency.
- This change enhances the understanding of the parameter's purpose as a constructor for TypedArray types.

commit.message:
Render conversation threads from data.messages

When a notification carries data.messages, display a rich
conversation thread instead of the plain body text:

- Each message shows a rounded bubble, sender name and avatar
  (or initials fallback) on the first message in each group
- Consecutive messages from the same sender are visually grouped:
  no repeated avatar/name after the first in each run
- Self-messages (no sender field) are right-aligned in green;
  others are left-aligned in the card's border colour
- senderIcon may be raw base64, a data URI, or a URL — all handled
- Plain body rendering is unchanged when data.messages is absent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: split release workflow into PR-based two-workflow pipeline

The original release.yml pushed directly to main, which fails due to
branch protection rules (GH013). Split into two workflows:

- release.yml: workflow_dispatch creates release/vX.Y.Z branch with
  version bump + changelog update, opens PR to main
- release-publish.yml: on merged release PR, creates tag + GitHub
  Release + deletes release branch

Also includes:
- Pin checkout to main in release.yml to prevent running from wrong branch
- Use merge_commit_sha in release-publish.yml to prevent race conditions
- Add semver validation in publish workflow
- Update CHANGELOG to reflect full scope of [Unreleased] additions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

build(deps-dev): Bump typescript from 5.9.3 to 6.0.2 (#4)

* build(deps-dev): Bump typescript from 5.9.3 to 6.0.2

Bumps [typescript](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript) from 5.9.3 to 6.0.2.
- [Release notes](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/compare/v5.9.3...v6.0.2)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: typescript
  dependency-version: 6.0.2
  dependency-type: direct:development
  update-type: version-update:semver-major
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

* build: update bun.lock for typescript 6.0.2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: split auto-fix into lockfile, format, and commit jobs

Split the single auto-format job into three independent jobs following
the patch-and-apply pattern from devantler-tech/ksail:

- update-lockfile: runs bun install, uploads bun.lock patch
- auto-format: runs prettier, uploads formatting patch (excludes bun.lock)
- auto-commit: downloads all patches, applies them, and commits

The lockfile and format jobs run in parallel with read-only permissions.
Only auto-commit needs write access and the GitHub App token.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add explicit types config for TypeScript 6 and fix zizmor tag comments

TypeScript 6 no longer auto-includes all @types/* packages. Add
explicit "types": ["bun"] to tsconfig.json to restore Bun and
Node.js type resolution.

Fix upload-artifact version comments from '# v7' to '# v7.0.0' to
match the actual tag for the pinned SHA, resolving zizmor findings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

[ci-coach] ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs (#9)

Both Solutions/JavaScript/The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos/ and
Solutions/Python/ contain self-contained test suites that were never
executed by CI — only syntax-checked. This change:

- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-javascript job:
  runs node The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos.test.js (no npm install needed)
- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-python job:
  runs python test_gridlock_arena.py (no pip install needed)
- Fixes Python validation to use find instead of shell glob for
  consistency with the JS job and to cover future subdirectories

All tests verified passing locally before this commit.

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive security hardening — full audit remediation

CRITICAL fixes:
- C-1: Payment fraud — verify eventId/eventId2 against Razorpay order notes
  server-side after signature check; also verify amount_paid >= expected
- C-2: Firestore rules — deny all client writes to students/registrations;
  move initial registration setDoc to server-side /api/auth/register endpoint
- C-3: Remove live OTP from email URL (was leaking to browser history/logs);
  OTP now displayed as text only, not in clickable autofill link

HIGH fixes:
- H-1: Replace Math.random() OTP generation with crypto.randomInt (CSPRNG)
- H-2: JWT getSessionFromRequest no longer falls back to empty string secret
- H-4: Add rate limits to admin destructive routes (reset: 3/hr, upload: 3/hr)
- H-5: Admin students PUT field allowlist (mass assignment protection)
- H-6: Admin config POST field allowlist — only known config keys accepted
- H-7: Payment create-order and verify both rate-limited 10/hr per user
- H-8: Email format/length validation added to send-otp (RFC 5322 regex)
- H-10: upload-csv validates each student record; max 5000 per upload
- H-11: notify/email rate-limited to 10/hr per user (Brevo quota protection)

MEDIUM fixes:
- M-2: JWT now includes issuer (chemnova-2026) and audience (chemnova-student)
  claims; all routes verify them on decode
- M-4: verify-otp validates OTP is a string matching /^\d{6}$/ before use;
  prevents attempt counter bypass via numeric JSON type
- M-5: slot parameter strictly validated as 1 or 2 in create-order and verify
- M-6: Email removed from localStorage SessionData (reduces XSS exposure)
- M-7: CSP removes unsafe-eval in production; only dev mode retains it;
  added Razorpay domains to script-src and connect-src

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix(integrations): Guard invalid HA timezone

Handle null, empty, and invalid timezone strings
from the HA config endpoint.  HAClient.get_timezone
now validates the value is a non-empty string
before returning it.  RentalControlService wraps
ZoneInfo construction in a try/except to fall back
to UTC when the string is not a valid IANA zone.

fix(ViewTransition): enable vertical scrolling on small screens

- Active layer uses position:relative so content defines document height
- Enter/exit layers use position:absolute (top/left/right, no bottom) with
  pointerEvents:none so they animate without affecting layout
- Container uses overflow-x:hidden only, freeing vertical scroll
- Reset window scroll to top on each view change

commit.message:
fix: include album directory in completed file paths for organiser

slskd stores completed downloads as <completed_dir>/<album_dir>/<filename>
but the path was constructed using only the filename component, causing
'Source file not found' warnings for every download.

Use parts[-2]/parts[-1] (same pattern as _cleanup_incomplete_files) to
include the album directory when resolving remote paths to local paths.

Wraps DOM lookups and event listeners in initSearch() driven by astro:page-load
event. Pagefind instance persists across navigations. Old event listeners torn
down before re-attaching. Global keydown listener registered once via guard flag.
Adds 2 Playwright regression tests for post-navigation search.
